title: galium helveticum subsp. tendae
intro: Galium helveticum subsp. tendae, also known as the alpine bedstraw or Swiss bedstraw, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent, due to the presence of iridoid glycosides and other bioactive compounds. Galium helveticum subsp. tendae has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from the leaves and flowers, and are available in forms such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
